user_query:
I'm coordinating Uncle Joe's surprise 60th and need help consolidating RSVPs from multiple sources and drafting messages to keep everyone aligned.

Inputs (all relative to the workspace):
- Guest list: input/guest_list.csv
- Event details: input/event_details.yaml
- Venue capacity: input/venue_capacity.txt
- RSVP sources: every CSV file inside input/rsvps/ (do not hardcode filenames; scan the directory and combine all CSVs you find there)

Please do the following and produce the specified outputs:
1) Consolidate RSVPs and export a deduplicated view:
   - Read all CSV files under input/rsvps/ and combine them.
   - Only consider invitees whose email exists in input/guest_list.csv.
   - Normalize response values case-insensitively to one of: Yes, No, Maybe.
   - If an invitee appears more than once across sources, keep only their single latest record by max timestamp_iso (ISO 8601 UTC).
   - Treat plus_ones as an integer; if missing, assume 0.
   - Write the deduplicated, latest-by-email set to output/working/latest_rsvp.csv with columns: email,response,plus_ones,timestamp_iso.

2) Compute coordination stats for the party and save summary files:
   - Using guest_list.csv and the deduplicated latest_rsvp.csv:
     • total_invited = number of rows in guest_list.csv.
     • total_responded = number of invitees with a latest RSVP.
     • yes_count, no_count, maybe_count = counts by response.
     • expected_headcount_yes_incl_plus_ones = sum over all Yes of (1 + plus_ones).
     • Read capacity from input/venue_capacity.txt (a single integer) and compute capacity_remaining = capacity − expected_headcount_yes_incl_plus_ones.
   - Write a single-row CSV to output/summary/attendance_summary.csv with exactly these columns: total_invited,total_responded,yes_count,no_count,maybe_count,expected_headcount_yes_incl_plus_ones,capacity,capacity_remaining.
   - Produce a per-group breakdown using the group field from guest_list.csv. For each distinct group, compute: invited_count, responded_count, yes_count, no_count, maybe_count, yes_headcount (sum of 1 + plus_ones for Yes in that group). Save this as output/summary/group_breakdown.csv with columns: group,invited_count,responded_count,yes_count,no_count,maybe_count,yes_headcount.
   - Identify invitees who have no RSVP at all (i.e., present in guest_list.csv but missing from latest_rsvp.csv). Write them to output/summary/unresponded.csv with columns: name,email,group.

3) Draft plain-text email messages:
   - For each Yes response in output/working/latest_rsvp.csv, create output/messages/confirmations/<email>.txt containing a short draft email that:
     • Starts with a "To: <email>" line and a "Subject:" line that includes the event_name from input/event_details.yaml.
     • Greets the guest by their first name (from input/guest_list.csv).
     • Confirms their attendance and mentions the total party size as (1 + plus_ones).
     • Includes date, time, and location from input/event_details.yaml, and a note to arrive arrival_buffer_minutes earlier than the start time (e.g., "Please arrive X minutes early so we can surprise him").
     • Reminds them to keep it a surprise.
     • Includes the organizer_name and contact_phone from input/event_details.yaml.
   - For each unresponded invitee (from output/summary/unresponded.csv), create output/messages/reminders/<email>.txt containing a short draft email that:
     • Starts with a "To: <email>" line and a "Subject:" line that includes the event_name and the word RSVP.
     • Greets the guest by their first name.
     • Politely asks them to RSVP, includes the date, time, and location, and reminds them to keep it a surprise.
     • Includes the organizer_name and contact_phone for easy reply.

Notes and constraints:
- Create any missing output directories.
- All paths above are required. Use only the latest RSVP per email when computing stats and generating messages.
- Only guests listed in input/guest_list.csv should appear in outputs.

Deliverables to verify:
- output/working/latest_rsvp.csv
- output/summary/attendance_summary.csv
- output/summary/group_breakdown.csv
- output/summary/unresponded.csv
- output/messages/confirmations/*.txt (one per Yes)
- output/messages/reminders/*.txt (one per unresponded) All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
